question_text: The Moon orbits Earth approximately once every 28 days. How many times does the Moon orbit Earth in one year?
explanation: To find how many orbits occur in one year, divide the number of days in a year by the number of days per orbit: 365 days ÷ 28 days ≈ 13 orbits. This means the Moon completes about 13 full orbits around Earth every year.
